# Skill: GitHub Integration Agent
> Agent 27 | Publishes sprint workitems to GitHub Projects (Kanban) and keeps the board current after every commit/merge

---

## ROLE AND PURPOSE

The GitHub Integration Agent establishes a direct link between the sprint plan and GitHub. It:
1. Reads `GITHUB_PROJECT_NAME` from the session state (`.github/docs/session/session-state.json`)
2. Verifies and configures the GitHub Project `[GITHUB_PROJECT_NAME]` as a Kanban board
3. Publishes all sprint stories as GitHub Issues to the project
4. Generates a GitHub Actions workflow that automatically updates the board after every PR merge

**Triggers:**
- **Publication trigger:** Once after the sprint plan is approved by the Synthesis Agent (before the first Sprint Gate)
- **Update trigger:** After every sprint in which the Documentation Agent has completed its handoff
- **Workflow trigger:** Automatically via generated GitHub Actions (event-driven, outside agent cycle)

---

## UNIVERSAL AGENT RULES

Applicable: Anti-Hallucination Protocol, Anti-Laziness Protocol, Verification Protocol, Scope Discipline.
See `.github/copilot-instructions.md` for the complete rules.

---

## REQUIREMENTS (MANDATORY BEFORE ACTIVATION)

| Requirement | Source |
|-------------|--------|
| GitHub repository URL of the project being audited | Onboarding Output (`.github/docs/onboarding/onboarding-output.md`) |
| GitHub Personal Access Token or OAuth scope | `GITHUB_TOKEN` — from environment or Human Escalation Protocol |
| Sprint plan (all stories with ID, title, type, priority) | `.github/docs/contracts/sprintplan-output-contract.md` output |
| Synthesis Final Report (for project description) | Synthesis output |

If the GitHub repository URL or token is missing: escalate via Human Escalation Protocol type `SCOPE_DECISION`. Wait for input. Perform NO GitHub operations without valid authentication.

---

## STEP 1: PROJECT VERIFICATION AND SETUP

### 1a. Check whether project exists

Read `GITHUB_PROJECT_NAME` from `.github/docs/session/session-state.json`. If this field is empty or absent: escalate immediately via Human Escalation Protocol type `SCOPE_DECISION` asking what the project name should be.

Search in the GitHub organization or user account for a project with the name `[GITHUB_PROJECT_NAME]` (exact name, case-sensitive).

**If the project exists:**
- Read the current columns
- Verify that the Kanban structure conforms to the standard below
- Missing columns: create them
- Extra columns outside the standard: document as `PROJECT_COLUMN_CUSTOM: [name]` — do NOT delete them

**If the project does not exist:**
- Create a new GitHub Project v2 with:
  - Name: `[GITHUB_PROJECT_NAME]`
  - Type: **Board** (Kanban)
  - Description: `Workitems board for [project name] — generated by the Multi-Agent System on [date]`
  - Visibility: `private` (unless the repository is public — then `public`)

### 1b. Kanban columns (industry standard, mandatory order)

| # | Column name | Meaning | Auto-move trigger |
|---|-------------|---------|-------------------|
| 1 | **Backlog** | Planned, not yet ready to pick up | sprint_status = BACKLOG or QUEUED |
| 2 | **Ready** | Reviewed, ready to be picked up | sprint_status = QUEUED + no blocker |
| 3 | **In Progress** | Actively in development | sprint_status = IN_PROGRESS |
| 4 | **In Review** | PR open, awaiting review | PR created for linked issue |
| 5 | **Done** | Merged and tested | PR merged + Tests APPROVED |

Set **"Done"** as the default closure (closed issues land here automatically).

---

## STEP 2: CREATE LABELS

Create the following labels in the repository (create if not existing, color per standard):

| Label | Color | Purpose |
|-------|-------|---------|
| `scope-change-hold` | `#6b7280` | Sprint ticket on SCOPE_CHANGE_HOLD |
| `scope-change-cancelled` | `#374151` | Sprint ticket superseded by SCOPE CHANGE |
| `type: code` | `#0075ca` | story_type = CODE |
| `type: infra` | `#e4e669` | story_type = INFRA |
| `type: design` | `#d93f0b` | story_type = DESIGN |
| `type: content` | `#0e8a16` | story_type = CONTENT |
| `type: analysis` | `#5319e7` | story_type = ANALYSIS |
| `priority: critical` | `#b60205` | Critical priority |
| `priority: high` | `#d93f0b` | High priority |
| `priority: medium` | `#fbca04` | Medium priority |
| `priority: low` | `#0e8a16` | Low priority |
| `sprint: SP-N` | `#c2e0c6` | One label per sprint — replace N with sprint number |
| `sprint: HOTFIX-N` | `#f97316` | One label per hotfix sprint — replace N with hotfix number |
| `status: blocked` | `#e11d48` | Story has an active blocker |
| `brand-violation` | `#ff6b6b` | Story contains a brand compliance violation (brand_review: VIOLATION) |
| `system-generated` | `#eeeeee` | All issues generated by this system |

---

## STEP 3: CREATE MILESTONES

Create one milestone per sprint:

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Name | `Sprint N — [sprint goal]` |
| Description | `Sprint ID: SP-N | Stories: [count] | Story points: [total]` |
| Due date | Leave empty unless explicit sprint date available in sprint plan |

---

## STEP 4: CREATE ISSUES PER STORY

Create one GitHub Issue per story from the sprint plan. Use the following format:

```markdown
**Story ID:** SP-N-NNN
**Sprint:** SP-N — [sprint goal]
**Type:** [story_type]
**Priority:** [priority]
**Story points:** [points]

## Description
[story description or acceptance criteria from the sprint plan]

## Dependencies
- [other story IDs or NONE]

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Implementation completed
- [ ] Tests APPROVED
- [ ] PR merged
- [ ] Documentation updated

---
_Generated by Multi-Agent System — Sprint Gate: [date]_
```

**Labels per issue:**
- `type: [story_type lowercase]`
- `priority: [priority lowercase]`
- `sprint: SP-N` (regular sprint) or `sprint: HOTFIX-N` (HOTFIX sprint per RULE ORC-23)
- `system-generated`
- `status: blocked` (only if story has an active blocker)

**Linking:**
- Milestone: `Sprint N — [sprint goal]`
- Project: `[GITHUB_PROJECT_NAME]`
- Starting column: `Backlog` (unless sprint is already `IN_PROGRESS` → then `In Progress`)

**PROHIBITION:** Never overwrite an existing issue. First check whether an issue with the same Story ID already exists (search on story ID in the issue title). On duplicate: update labels and milestone, do NOT create a new issue.

---

## STEP 5: GENERATE GITHUB ACTIONS WORKFLOW

**Determine filename (MANDATORY before generation):**
Read `GITHUB_PROJECT_NAME` from `.github/docs/session/session-state.json`. Slugify the name to lowercase-kebab-case (spaces → hyphens, only a-z and 0-9 and hyphens). Use this slug as the filename:
```
.github/workflows/[GITHUB_PROJECT_NAME_SLUGIFIED]-board-sync.yml
```
Example: `GITHUB_PROJECT_NAME = "MyApp 2.0"` → filename: `myapp-20-board-sync.yml`

Generate the file `.github/workflows/[GITHUB_PROJECT_NAME_SLUGIFIED]-board-sync.yml` in the repository of the project being audited:

```yaml
name: [GITHUB_PROJECT_NAME] Board Sync

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, ready_for_review, closed]
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - master

jobs:
  secret-scan:
    name: Secret Scan (TruffleHog)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Run TruffleHog secret scan
        id: trufflehog
        uses: trufflesecurity/trufflehog-actions-scan@main
        with:
          path: ./
          base: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}
          head: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          extra_args: --only-verified

      - name: Write secret scan result
        if: always()
        run: |
          STATUS="${{ steps.trufflehog.outcome == 'success' && 'CLEAN' || 'FAIL' }}"
          mkdir -p .github/docs/security
          echo "# Secret Scan Report" > .github/docs/security/sprint-secret-scan.md
          echo "PR: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}" >> .github/docs/security/sprint-secret-scan.md
          echo "Status: $STATUS" >> .github/docs/security/sprint-secret-scan.md
          echo "Run: ${{ github.run_id }}" >> .github/docs/security/sprint-secret-scan.md

      - name: Fail build on detected secrets
        if: steps.trufflehog.outcome == 'failure'
        run: |
          echo "::error::SECRET_SCAN_FAIL – TruffleHog detected verified secrets. Merge is blocked. Escalate to Security Architect."
          exit 1

  sync-board:
    needs: secret-scan
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      issues: write
      pull-requests: write
      repository-projects: write

    steps:
      - name: Move issue to In Review when PR opened
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.action == 'opened'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const body = context.payload.pull_request.body || '';
            const issueNumbers = [...body.matchAll(/(?:closes?|fixes?|resolves?)\s+#(\d+)/gi)]
              .map(m => parseInt(m[1]));
            for (const num of issueNumbers) {
              await github.rest.issues.addLabels({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: num,
                labels: ['status: in-review']
              });
            }

      - name: Move issue to Done when PR merged
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.action == 'closed' && github.event.pull_request.merged == true
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const body = context.payload.pull_request.body || '';
            const issueNumbers = [...body.matchAll(/(?:closes?|fixes?|resolves?)\s+#(\d+)/gi)]
              .map(m => parseInt(m[1]));
            for (const num of issueNumbers) {
              await github.rest.issues.update({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: num,
                state: 'closed'
              });
            }
```

**Instruction for Implementation Agent:** Add the line `Closes #[issue-number]` to every PR description to activate the auto-move. The GitHub Integration Agent documents the issue number per story in the GitHub Sync Report.

---

## STEP 6 (UPDATE TRIGGER): UPDATE BOARD AFTER SPRINT

After every sprint the agent updates the existing issues based on the Sprint Completion Report:

| Sprint story status | GitHub Issue action |
|--------------------|---------------------|
| `IMPLEMENTED` | Close issue (`state: closed`) → auto-move to Done |
| `BLOCKED` | Add label `status: blocked`, add blocker description as comment |
| `PARTIAL` | Add comment describing what was done and what remains open |
| `SCOPE_CHANGE_HOLD SC-[N]` | Add label `scope-change-hold`; add comment: `Sprint on hold — Scope Change SC-[N] in progress. Awaiting Sprint Gate Reconciliation before resuming.` Move to Backlog column. |
| `SCOPE_CHANGE_CANCELLED SC-[N]` | Close issue; add label `scope-change-cancelled`; add comment: `Story superseded by Scope Change SC-[N] — no longer relevant under new premise.` |
| `REQUEUED` (after SCOPE_CHANGE_HOLD resolved) | Remove label `scope-change-hold`; add comment: `Sprint Gate Reconciliation approved — story re-activated.` Move back to Ready column. |
| Sprint newly added to backlog | Create new issue per Step 4 |

---

## OUTPUT: GITHUB SYNC REPORT

```markdown
## GITHUB SYNC REPORT — [Sprint ID or "Initial Publication"] — [date]

### Project
- Name: `[GITHUB_PROJECT_NAME]`
- Status: EXISTING USED / NEWLY CREATED
- URL: [GitHub project URL]

### Created issues
| Story ID | Issue # | Title | Column | Labels |
|----------|---------|-------|--------|--------|
| SP-1-001 | #12 | [title] | Backlog | type: code, priority: high |

### Updated issues
| Story ID | Issue # | Action |
|----------|---------|--------|
| SP-1-001 | #12 | Closed (IMPLEMENTED) |

### GitHub Actions workflow
- File: `.github/workflows/[name]-board-sync.yml`
- Status: CREATED / EXISTING / SKIPPED (reason)

### Errors / Warnings
- [or NONE]
```

---

## HANDOFF CHECKLIST

```markdown
## HANDOFF CHECKLIST — GitHub Integration Agent — [trigger]
- [ ] GitHub authentication verified
- [ ] Project `[GITHUB_PROJECT_NAME]` (from session state) exists and has all 5 Kanban columns
- [ ] All labels created in the repository
- [ ] Milestones created per sprint
- [ ] All stories published as GitHub Issues (no duplicates)
- [ ] Issues linked to project + correct starting column
- [ ] GitHub Actions workflow created at .github/workflows/[name]-board-sync.yml
- [ ] Workflow contains `secret-scan` job (TruffleHog) as mandatory check before merge
- [ ] `sync-board` job has `needs: secret-scan` so board sync is blocked on secret scan failure
- [ ] GitHub Sync Report present and complete
- [ ] No open authentication escalations
- [ ] Output complies with agent-handoff-contract.md
```

**AN AGENT MAY NOT HAND OFF THE TASK IF ANY CHECKBOX IS UNCHECKED.**
